# Town of Wiley AI Context

This file provides compiled AI-development context for the Town of Wiley repository.

## Repository Overview

Town of Wiley is an Angular 21 web application backed by AWS infrastructure. The frontend is built around standalone Angular APIs, signals-oriented state, native control flow, SCSS styling, Playwright end-to-end tests, and Vitest/browser unit coverage.

## Authoritative Project Guidance

The following files are the primary sources of truth for AI-assisted work in this repository:

- `.vscode/angular-best-practices.md`
- `.vscode/llms.txt`
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
- `.instructions.md`
- `.github/instructions/ai-application-development.instructions.md`

When there is overlap, prefer `.vscode/angular-best-practices.md` for Angular code standards.

## Angular Standards

- Use standalone components instead of NgModules.
- Do not add `standalone: true` to Angular decorators in Angular v20+.
- Prefer signals for local state and `computed()` for derived state.
- Prefer `input()` and `output()` over decorator-based inputs and outputs.
- Prefer `inject()` over constructor injection when practical.
- Set `changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` on components.
- Prefer native control flow blocks (`@if`, `@for`, `@switch`) over `*ngIf`, `*ngFor`, and `*ngSwitch`.
- Prefer reactive forms instead of template-driven forms.
- Avoid `ngClass` and `ngStyle`; use class and style bindings instead.
- Use `NgOptimizedImage` for static images when applicable.

## Accessibility Standards

- Code must pass AXE checks where applicable.
- Code must meet WCAG AA minimums, including keyboard access, focus management, readable contrast, semantics, and appropriate ARIA usage.
- Favor semantic HTML and accessible user-facing locators in test code.

## Testing And Validation

- Use Angular and workspace-native validation paths before ad hoc commands when available.
- Main frontend validation surfaces include:
  - `npm run build`
  - `npm run lint`
  - `npm run test:unit`
  - `npm run test:vitest`
  - `npm run test:e2e:smoke`
  - `npm run test:e2e:snapshots`
- Playwright coverage and snapshot baselines are already part of this repository.

## AI Tooling Setup

- Angular CLI MCP server is configured in `.vscode/mcp.json`.
- PrimeNG MCP server is configured in `.vscode/mcp.json`.
- Vitest MCP server is configured in `.vscode/mcp.json`.
- Copilot instruction files are enabled in `.vscode/settings.json`.
- Chat agents and memory tooling are enabled in `.vscode/settings.json`.
- An AI feature development instruction file is available at `.github/instructions/ai-application-development.instructions.md`.
- An AI feature specialist agent and prompt are available under `.github/agents/ai-feature.agent.md` and `.github/prompts/ai-feature.prompt.md`.

## Web Codegen Scorer

- Web Codegen Scorer is installed in the local environment and available on PATH.
- Repository scorer configuration is defined in `hello-world/config.mjs`.
- The scorer is configured for Angular and uses `.vscode/angular-best-practices.md` as its generation system prompt.

## Working Guidance For AI Tools

- Use repository-local Angular guidance before generic Angular examples.
- Use Angular CLI MCP for Angular documentation, best practices, examples, and workspace inspection when available.
- Keep model credentials out of client-delivered code and prefer repo secret flows or server-side boundaries for privileged AI access.
- Constrain tool-calling integrations with explicit allowlists and validate model-requested actions before execution.
- Design AI-backed user flows for non-determinism with schema validation, safe fallbacks, and graceful degradation.
- Keep changes small, consistent with the existing codebase, and focused on user-facing correctness.
- Preserve accessibility, responsive behavior, and deterministic tests when modifying UI.
